mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and management of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators looking to automate cluster lifecycle management across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of initial setup and the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

```

> This repository contains the necessary charts for installing Cluster API.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are required for Cluster API to function properly. This step installs them into your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml

```

> Ensure you have the correct version of CRDs for your Cluster API version.

steps.title: Install Cluster API components
steps.description:
This command installs the Cluster API components into the specified namespace.

```bash

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version v1.12.4

```

> This installs the Cluster API components in the kube-system namespace.
commands: helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version v1.12.4

steps.title: Create a Cluster API custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Cluster API custom resource to manage your Kubernetes clusters.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructure
    name: my-infrastructure
EOF

```

> Replace 'MyInfrastructure' with your actual infrastructure provider.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructure
    name: my-infrastructure
EOF

steps.title: Verify Cluster API installation
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Cluster API components to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> You should see the Cluster API components running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API components
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Cluster API components and CRDs from your cluster. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if clusters managed by CAPI are not deleted first.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace kube-system

kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml

```

> This removes the CRDs associated with Cluster API.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your current cluster state. This step will upgrade the Cluster API components to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.status.phase == "Running") | .metadata.name' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version v1.12.4

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> Drain all nodes to prepare for upgrade.
> This upgrades the Cluster API components.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n kube-system -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.status.phase == "Running") | .metadata.name' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data
helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version v1.12.4

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Cluster" in version "cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster CRD has not been installed or is not available.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the CRDs are installed correctly by running the CRD installation command again.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: unable to create infrastructure'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error typically occurs when the infrastructure provider is not configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Check your infrastructure provider configuration and ensure all required credentials are set.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in kube-system are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen due to misconfiguration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kube-system' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates a problem with the kubeconfig or the API server certificate.

**Fix:**
Verify your kubeconfig file and ensure that the API server certificate is valid.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new infrastructure providers and improved error handling.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old API version 'v1alpha3' for Cluster resources.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.11.x, ensure to migrate your Cluster resources to the new API version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n kube-system' showing all Cluster API components in 'Running' state.
codeSnippets: helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version v1.12.4

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4
    kind: MyInfrastructure
    name: my-infrastructure
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.26 or higher and the necessary tools installed.
